description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rough, fractured surface and a predominantly gray coloration. The rock displays several visible cracks and angular edges, suggesting it has broken off from a larger formation. On one face, there are black alphanumeric markings, likely applied with paint or marker, and a white label or sticker is also visible. The surface texture varies from coarse and grainy to slightly smoother patches, with some areas showing a faint purplish hue. The rock appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban environment.
description: A rough, gray rock with angular edges, visible cracks, and black markings on one face. It has a dense, solid appearance with some purplish and white patches.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the rock, featuring black alphanumeric markings and a white label on a rough, fractured gray surface.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the rock's vertical cracks and angular profile, with less visible markings and a more uniform gray coloration.
description_view_2: This view reveals a large, purplish-gray surface with a white patch and a small exposed mineral inclusion, lacking visible markings.
description_view_3: This angle shows a narrower, jagged side with deep cracks and a darker gray tone, with minimal visible markings.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
